seoTitle: Configure Multiple Choice Questions in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Build and customize Choice questions in Microsoft Forms, including multi-select toggles, shuffled options, and a write-in Other field.
intro: Multiple-choice questions are the backbone of most Forms surveys, but getting the most out of them means knowing how to enable multi-select answers, add a write-in option, and randomize the order. This guide covers configuring a Choice question beyond the basics.

steps.heading: Add a Choice question
steps.body: Click Add New and select the Choice option tile from the field menu.

steps.heading: Write your prompt and options
steps.body: Enter a specific, descriptive question, then fill in each option row underneath it, using Add Option to add more.

steps.heading: Enable multi-select if needed
steps.body: Slide the Multiple Answers toggle on to swap radio buttons for checkboxes when more than one answer should be allowed.

steps.heading: Add a write-in option
steps.body: Tap Add "Other" to insert a text field so participants can type a personalized answer not listed in your options.

steps.heading: Shuffle and require
steps.body: Use the three-dot More Settings menu to toggle Shuffle Options for randomized answer order, and toggle Required to prevent skipped questions.

faq.question: How do I let respondents pick more than one answer?
faq.answer: Toggle Multiple Answers to switch the question from radio buttons to checkboxes.

faq.question: Can respondents write in an answer that isn't listed?
faq.answer: Yes, adding the Other option inserts a smart text field for personalized write-in responses.

faq.question: Should I randomize answer order?
faq.answer: Shuffling options can reduce order bias, which is useful for surveys where answer position might influence responses.

faq.question: Can I delete an option after adding it?
faq.answer: Yes, individual option rows can be removed from the question at any time before publishing.
